_comment: HELD-OUT TEST corpus — the CI quality gate scores against this, not the tuning corpus. RULE: do not edit the lexicon to make a specific note here pass; that turns the test set back into a training set (the whole point of improvement #4). Notes deliberately include the failure classes the old 45-note corpus never tested: negated positives, low/high numeric scores, and true-negative / idiom notes that must produce NOTHING. Caveat: authored by one person for now; when real beta data exists, resample this from held-out real observations with a second annotator (improvement #9).

notes.raw: Emma did not do well on the assessment today
Noah was not confident and made no improvement
Aisha could not stay on task during the lesson
Diego got 3 out of 10 on the spelling quiz
Liam scored 40% on the number check
Marcus got 9 out of 10 correct on the sight word test
During the transition to lunch the class lined up
Read the sentence on the board aloud
Field trip permission slips are due Monday
Picture day is on Friday so no academics today
Sophia segmented the word into onset and rime
Liam used the ten frame to show the number
Diego took turns with a friend at the table
Noah followed the two step direction the first time
Aisha balanced on the balance beam without help
Liam sounded out the word using an anchor chart
Emma lined up without a verbal prompt
Diego stayed on task during independent work
Marcus wrote a full sentence by himself
the class sang the counting song at circle time
